title: calochortus catalinae
intro: Calochortus catalinae, also known as the Santa Cruz mariposa lily, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the coastal mountains of central California. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with roots and flowers being used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Calochortus catalinae have been reported to include antiviral and antimicrobial activities, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the roots and flowers, and can be used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
